span.p:
Does the build need private network
or special hardware GPU/ARM/large?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Self-hosted / larger runners —
ephemeral, auto-scaled, hardened

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Provider-hosted runners —
no infra to maintain, default choice

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Is the runner reused across jobs?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Persistent runners leak state between
builds — prefer ephemeral; if persistent,
clean the workspace + cache deliberately

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Cache deps keyed on the lockfile hash
with a restore-key fallback

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Multi-stage Dockerfile with
slow layers above fast layers?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Reorder Dockerfile first; then
registry-backed layer cache / BuildKit cache

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Registry/remote build cache —
cache-from/cache-to a shared store

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Remote/distributed build cache
e.g. content-addressed artifact cache
